text.title: core-vui-design-principles Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for core-vui-design-principles as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLM generates verbose, apologetic responses despite instructions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Add a stop-list to the system prompt and enforce max word count in post-processing — models do not reliably self-edit on tone alone.

failure-mode.description: Context-awareness regressions are silent — the agent answers but ignores the prior turn.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always include the last 3-5 turns verbatim in the prompt and assert the response references at least one entity from them in the critic pass.

failure-mode.description: LLM counts words or characters to enforce length limits.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs are unreliable at counting; enforce length limits in code post-generation, not via model instruction.

failure-mode.description: Single agent decides both "is auth required?" and "speak the response" for sensitive operations.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Split into a policy agent and a response agent; the executor must never see raw sensitive values. See vui-privacy-security methodology for the full pattern.
